channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to a fraud charge related to duping donors who gave money to a private effort to build a wall along the U.S. southern border — a case the conservative strategist has decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dogs from great Danes to terriers head into the ring at Westminster show
item.description: Big dogs are having their day at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show, where a bunch of massive breeds are being judged Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Criminal case against NYC Mayor hangs by a thread after Justice Department orders it dropped
item.description: The criminal case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is hanging by a thread after the Justice Department ordered federal prosecutors to drop the charges.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz And Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Salman Rushdie testifies about his shock and pain as a stranger repeatedly stabbed him on stage
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — Salman Rushdie described in graphic detail Tuesday the frenzied moments in 2022 when a masked man rushed at him on a stage in western New York and repeatedly slashed him with a knife, leaving him with life-threatening injuries.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump won't block immigration arrests in houses of worship. Now these 27 religious groups are suing
item.description: More than two-dozen Christian and Jewish groups representing millions of Americans — ranging from the Episcopal Church and the Union for Reform Judaism to the Mennonites and Unitarian Universalists — filed a federal court lawsuit Tuesday challenging a Trump administration move giving immigration age
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crush of Valentine's Day flowers: Nearly a billion go through this city to you
item.description: If any husbands or boyfriends mess up Valentine's Day this week, it's not because of a shortage of flowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canned tuna sold at Walmart, Trader Joe's, Costco, others is recalled in multiple states, including Illinois
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a recall on canned tuna products sold at several different retailers including Walmart, Trader Joe's, Kroger, and Costco.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9

item.title: No, your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE illegally accessed your data
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos claim otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams to address media as DOJ orders charges to be dropped
item.description: The Justice Department ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against the New York City mayor.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about proposals to ban abortion pills and punish women who seek abortion
item.description: Lawmakers in some states are pushing measures to crack down on abortion pills or penalize women who obtain abortions.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What the Westminster show dogs' names mean
item.description: They have names that could make a random password generator cry uncle. Meet, for example, GCHG CH Calicops Sassafras Gonnakikurass. “She’s a saucy girl. Her name says it all,” Fred Ortiz said as he groomed the Brussels griffon to compete Monday at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela sends 2 planes to US to return migrants, signaling a potential improvement in relations
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Two Venezuelan planes flew to the United States on Monday and returned home with deported Venezuelans, signaling a possible improvement in relations between longtime diplomatic adversaries and a victory for President Donald Trump in his efforts to get more countries to take the
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Grenell as interim Kennedy Center executive director
item.description: President Trump on Monday named Richard Grenell, his envoy for special missions, the interim executive director of the Kennedy Center days after he named himself chair of a new board. “I am pleased to announce that Ric Grenell will serve as the Interim Executive Director of The Kennedy Center.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Hawaii court rules against insurance companies in Maui wildfire, allowing $4B settlement to proceed
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii’s Supreme Court ruled Monday that insurance companies can’t bring their own legal actions against those blamed for Maui’s catastrophic 2023 wildfire, allowing a $4 billion settlement that was on that the verge of collapse to proceed.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trader Joe's, Smith's limit daily egg purchases due to nationwide shortages
item.description: Trader Joe's is limiting egg sales to one dozen per customer daily -- and it is not the only company doing so.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: US judge keeps Trump plan to push out federal workers on hold
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's plan to downsize the federal workforce remains on hold after a courtroom hearing on Monday afternoon. It's the latest example of how the Republican president's ambitious plans have become ensnared in the judicial system. U.S. District Judge George O’Toole Jr.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump warns of ending Israel-Hamas ceasefire if all hostages aren't returned
item.description: President Trump on Monday suggested he might end a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas if all hostages being held in Gaza are not released by the end of the week, warning that if the deal does not go through, “all hell is going to break out.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: FEMA says it's halting payments for migrant housing in New York after Musk blasts money for hotels
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The acting head of the federal agency responsible for responding to disasters said Monday that he’s suspending payments sent to New York City to house migrants and that staff who made them will be held accountable, after Elon Musk blasted the transactions on his social media platfo
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s Department of Homeland Security looking to deputize IRS agents for immigration enforcement
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem sent a letter to U.S. Treasury secretary Scott Bessent, asking that IRS agents be provided to assist with immigration and enforcement efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs off on new steel and aluminum tariffs
item.description: President Trump on Monday signed proclamations to bolster tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the United States and crack down on attempts by China and Russia to evade penalties. Trump in 2018 announced 25 percent tariffs on steel and 10 percent tariffs on aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Confusion after emergency alert sent to phones in San Francisco
item.description: (KRON) -- Some cell phone users in San Francisco received an emergency alert Monday afternoon. Phones sounded the emergency alert tone after a notification said that an endangered adult was missing.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: American tourists attacked by shark at luxe Bahamas resort area
item.description: Police are investigating after two female American tourists were apparently involved in a shark attack at a popular Bahamas resort over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Catalytic converters stolen from Illinois district's school buses, forcing students to stay home
item.description: A suburban Illinois school district was forced to hold an emergency e-learning day after the catalytic converters were stolen from "over 80%" of its buses.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, NewsNation

item.title: Illegal alien charged in sandwich shop owner's murder caught and released into US under Biden: ICE
item.description: An illegal alien from the Dominican Republic has been charged with the home invasion murder of a Massachusetts sandwich shop owner named Ilias "Louie" Mavros.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Second sinkhole opens up on I-80 in New Jersey, shutting down roadway
item.description: A 40-foot by 40-foot hole opened up at the same exit on Dec. 26, caused by the collapse of an abandoned mineshaft underneath, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Alexa Farrell, NewsNation

item.title: Protester speaks after being hit by car during Salt Lake City demonstration
item.description: Padilla-Oliver said the driver slowed down and stopped, after which she "stood with pride" and held her sign.
item.dc:creator: MJ Jewkes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Palestinians wouldn't have right to return to developed Gaza
item.description: "No, they wouldn't, because they're going to have much better housing. Much better," Trump told Fox News's Bret Baier in an interview.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump pardons former Governor Rod Blagojevich
item.description: President Trump is reportedly set to pardon former Illinois governor Rod Blagojevich, who was convicted of crimes including seeking to sell an appointment to Barack Obama's old Senate seat and trying to shake down a children's hospital.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Combat your post-Super Bowl fatigue with these freebies, discounts
item.description: Even though the Super Bowl is over, several restaurants and chains are offering freebies to help football fans recover from Sunday. Here’s what you need to know. Starbucks The coffee chain is offering its rewards members a free tall hot or iced brewed coffee any time during “Starbucks Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Is the Super Bowl halftime singer lip syncing? Check for these signs
item.description: Casual fans will be watching the theatrics, choreography and guest appearances during Kendrick Lamar and SZA's Super Bowl halftime show. Sleuths will be watching their throats.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says 25 percent tariffs on steel and aluminum coming Monday
item.description: Trump said that the steel and aluminum tariffs would impact “everybody” when asked what countries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: President Trump arrives at Super Bowl LIX in New Orleans
item.description: In addition to the history that may be made on the field, the U.S. will see a first during Super Bowl LIX: a sitting president will be in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Trump official orders consumer protection agency to stop work
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5
